_comment: v0.8 game-specific guides. HONESTY (v0.8): the Tier-1 entries here are AUTHORED starting points — the former 'Verified by N players' labels were not backed by real device tests, so they have been softened to 'Authored — try it, not yet device-verified'. Tier-3 entries pull from public EmuReady resources (each carries a real sourceUrl). All override base per-emulator guides via the resolver (picks lowest tier). dataAsOf: 2026-06-05.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install patched NetherSX2.
PS2 BIOS required. Place it in the bios folder. This comes from a PS2 you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
Kingdom Hearts 1 runs flawlessly on these settings.
Add your KH1 .iso/.chd.
Authored recommendation: locked 60 fps throughout. Use Vulkan; OpenGL is slower here.
Launch and enjoy — rock-solid performance on modern flagships.

troubleshooting.fix: Lower internal resolution to 2x if needed; GPU memory is the culprit.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install patched NetherSX2.
PS2 BIOS required. Place it in the bios folder. This comes from a PS2 you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
Persona 4 emulation settings — authored starting points, not yet device-verified.
Add your Persona 4 .iso/.chd.
Authored estimate (not device-verified): 4x IR runs flawlessly. Turn-based combat has zero frame drops.
Save after every day (in-game). One save per day takes ~30-40 seconds due to emulator overhead.
Launch and settle in for a long, perfect playthrough.

troubleshooting.fix: Ensure you're using Vulkan and have at least 8 GB free space. Saves are large.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install Eden.
Switch keys (prod.keys) and firmware are required. These come from a Nintendo Switch you own — there's no download for them. Import them via Eden's key/firmware installer once you have them.
Install Turnip 24.3.0 — crucial for Splatoon's shader pipeline.
Splatoon 3 container settings — Vulkan is stable.
Add your dumped Splatoon 3 .nsp/.xci + the latest update.
Authored tip (not device-verified): offline Salmon Run works perfectly. Build the shader cache in a few quick runs, then ranked matches are smooth ~45-50 fps.
Install game + update, let shaders compile, then play.

troubleshooting.fix: Normal — shader compilation on first sight. Async cache speeds subsequent matches significantly.
Confirm you have the game UPDATE installed; base game alone will crash.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install PPSSPP.
Crisis Core settings — real-time combat demands accuracy.
Add your Crisis Core .iso/.cso.
Authored estimate (not device-verified): locked 60 fps throughout. No slowdown even in boss fights.
Launch and dive in — flawless performance.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install Dolphin.
GameCube firmware is optional but recommended. This comes from a GameCube you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
Metroid Prime is GPU-heavy; tune carefully.
Add your Metroid Prime .iso/.gcm.
Authored recommendation: 2x IR holds solid 50-60 fps. Lock resolution or frame drops are noticeable.
Launch and explore — a classic First-Person Adventure masterpiece.

troubleshooting.fix: Disable 'Per-Pixel Lighting' if you see artifacts; standard lighting still looks great.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install Mobox.
Dead Cells is a 2D action-roguelike — runs lightly.
Copy your Dead Cells install.
Launch DeadCells.exe — locked 60 fps at 720p or higher.
Authored estimate (not device-verified): flawless roguelike loop. Fast load times, zero stutters.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install Mobox.
Tunic is a gorgeous indie 3D action-adventure. Runs great.
Copy your Tunic install.
Launch Tunic.exe at 720p. Expect 40-55 fps steady.
Community feedback: the pixel-art aesthetic holds up perfectly. Very stable overall.

troubleshooting.fix: Tunic's foliage is GPU-intensive. Lower shadow distance or cap 30 fps for smoothness.

guides.sourceLabel: Authored — try it, not yet device-verified

steps.text: Install patched NetherSX2.
PS2 BIOS required. Place it in the bios folder. This comes from a PS2 you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
Final Fantasy X on NetherSX2 is a showpiece.
Add your FFX .iso/.chd.
Authored estimate (not device-verified): 60 fps throughout. Cutscenes and battles all locked.
Sphere Grid animations are smooth; turn-based combat feels native.
Launch — a stunning, definitive FFX experience.

troubleshooting.fix: Rare, but if it occurs, drop to 2x IR temporarily.

steps.text: Install Eden.
Switch keys (prod.keys) and firmware are required. These come from a Nintendo Switch you own — there's no download for them. Import them via Eden's key/firmware installer once you have them.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Tears of the Kingdom container settings. This is a demanding title.
Add your dumped TotK .nsp/.xci + the latest updates.
Expect 25-40 fps in the open world. More stable (45-55) in dungeons. Not the smoothest, but absolutely playable.
Install all updates, let shaders compile, then explore Hyrule.

troubleshooting.fix: Drop to 0.5x resolution; big settlements are the bottleneck.
Confirm all game updates are installed — TotK is very patchy.

steps.text: Install GameNative.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Elden Ring DLC (Shadow of the Erdtree) is heavier than the base game. Tune down.
Play OFFLINE. EasyAntiCheat blocks online and risks a ban on emulation.
Copy your Elden Ring install (base game + DLC). Use the offline launcher.
DLC areas (Messmer's realm, Scadutree, etc.) demand lower settings than base game. 20-30 fps is realistic; cap it for stability.
Launch offline at 720p Low. DLC is for patient players.

troubleshooting.fix: Expected; drop to 540p Low and cap 24 fps.
Confirm VKD3D is on and video memory is set to max.

steps.text: Install PPSSPP.
Final Fantasy VII: Crisis Core on PSP emulation.
Add your Crisis Core .iso/.cso.
Launch — PPSSPP handles this perfectly at high res.
Install Dolphin.
GameCube firmware optional. This comes from a GameCube you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
Super Mario Sunshine — one of Dolphin's best runs.
Add your Sunshine .iso/.gcm.
2x holds 60 fps; 3x dips to 40-50 in water-heavy areas.
Launch and clean Isle Delfino.

troubleshooting.fix: Disable 'Per-Pixel Lighting'; Sunshine's water is notoriously finicky in emulation.

steps.text: Install patched NetherSX2.
PS2 BIOS required. Place it in the bios folder. This comes from a PS2 you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
Burnout Revenge racing settings.
Add your Burnout Revenge .iso/.chd.
Locked 60 fps racing. The crash mechanics are smooth.
Launch and cause traffic chaos.
Install GameHub.
Install Turnip 24.3.0.
Fallout: New Vegas on GameHub. This is the classic Vegas experience.
Copy your New Vegas install.
Launch at 720p Medium. Expect 30-45 fps.

troubleshooting.fix: Lower audio quality in the game settings; compression helps under emulation.

steps.text: Install RetroArch with the melonDS core.
Zelda: Phantom Hourglass — touch-screen adventure.
Add your Phantom Hourglass .nds.
Launch — melonDS handles the dual-screen perfectly.
Install patched NetherSX2.
PS2 BIOS required. Place it in the bios folder. This comes from a PS2 you own — there's no download here; place it once you have it.
GTA III — the first 3D GTA. Small, runs perfectly.
Add your GTA III .iso/.chd.
Locked 60 fps. A timeless open-world classic.
Launch and wreak havoc in Liberty City.
